priority_order: Implement a single optimized_lora.cu using raw cuBLAS SGEMM calls on the current PyTorch CUDA stream, with no ATen mm/matmul/addmm for the main computation.

Use the exact row-major to column-major reference mapping: SGEMM1 opA=N/opB=N A=X B=W m=d n=d k=d writes Y; SGEMM2 opA=N/opB=T A=X B=B m=d n=16 k=d writes U shaped {d,16} with ldc=d; SGEMM3 opA=N/opB=N A=U B=A m=d n=d k=16 accumulates into Y with beta=1.
Add robust runtime checks for CUDA float32 contiguous tensors, expected shapes W/X dxd and A/B dx16, same device, and d in [3584,4608]; allocate Y as {d,d} and U as {d,16} on the same device.
llm_advice.rationale: The only prior candidate was an ATen reference path with exact correctness but no speedup and explicit transpose materialization. The next lowest-risk optimization is the mandated pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM implementation, eliminating ATen launch overhead and avoiding B.T materialization while preserving the main dxd GEMM in cuBLAS. The specified SGEMM mapping directly matches PyTorch row-major storage through cuBLAS column-major reinterpretation and should be validated across the full runtime d range before considering any more aggressive variants.
risk_controls: Do not promote unless correctness passes for all tested d values, especially 3584, 4096, and 4608.
Bind cuBLAS to at::cuda::getCurrentCUDAStream() before issuing SGEMMs.
Avoid B.transpose(...).contiguous(), ATen mm/matmul/addmm, and any custom CUDA kernel in this candidate.
Use U allocation with row-major torch shape {d,16}, but pass ldc=d for its column-major interpretation.
Use alpha=1.0 for all SGEMMs; use beta=0.0 for SGEMM1 and SGEMM2; use beta=1.0 for SGEMM3.
Check cuBLAS status after handle creation, stream setup, and every SGEMM call.
Keep the existing ATen baseline as fallback if compile, static review, correctness, or benchmark fails.
Do not hardcode a single d or branch on hidden benchmark-only shapes.
stop_conditions: Stop and keep baseline if the cuBLAS candidate fails static review, compilation, or correctness on any required d.
Stop investigating alternative layouts if the reference U shape {d,16} mapping is correct and gives measurable speedup.
If speedup remains approximately 1.0x after a correct pure cuBLAS implementation, benchmark evidence should guide only low-risk cuBLAS-level mutations, not custom full GEMM kernels.
Do not explore handwritten CUDA low-rank kernels until the pure three-SGEMM cuBLAS reference candidate has been validated.

notes: Use ATen/cuBLAS for the d x d GEMM.
Explore kernels that fuse Y += A @ T for rank 16.
